reference: I am afraid I have dementia. I am fifty-nine years old. Around fifteen years ago, I developed problems with remembering the right words. My doctors did not think it was important. Now I mess up words constantly and lose things a lot. A few years ago, my brain magnetic resonance imaging showed a white matter lesion or something, but no one thought it was a big deal. Does this sound like Alzheimer’s disease?
text: I am afraid I have dementia. I am fifty-nine years old. Around fifteen years ago, I developed problems with remembering the right words. My doctors did not think it was important. Now I mess up words constantly and lose things a lot. Years ago, a brain magnetic resonance imaging showed a white matter lesion or something. But no one thought it was a big deal because it sounded like Alzheimer's disease.
